buildings.name: Maize Chinampa
buildings.description: Raised canal-fed maize plots. The estate food base and the first real production engine.

buildings.name: Cacao Garden
buildings.description: Protected garden plots for cacao. Valuable for ritual, nobles, tribute and market pressure.

buildings.name: Cotton Chinampa
buildings.description: Cotton plots that feed the cloth and fine textile chains.

buildings.name: Woodcutting Yard
buildings.description: A managed woodland work yard that brings timber into the estate economy. It belongs under Production > Workshops because it is a work site rather than a chinampa, but it is staffed by ordinary Field Labour rather than Tolteca artisans.

buildings.name: Obsidian Quarry Access
buildings.description: Quarry access and rough obsidian extraction for the estate. It appears under Production > Workshops because it is a specialised work site, but it uses ordinary Field Labour rather than craftsmen.

buildings.name: Basic Tool Workshop
buildings.description: A small starter workshop that prevents the estate from becoming tool-paralysed.

buildings.name: Cloth Workshop
buildings.description: Converts cotton and tools into cloth for upkeep, buildings and palace-facing production.

buildings.name: Weapon Yard
buildings.description: Produces weapons from obsidian, wood, cloth and tools. This is expensive but opens war readiness.

buildings.name: Fine Textile House
buildings.description: Produces elite palace-facing fine textiles from cotton, cacao and tools.

buildings.name: Small Field Labour Houses
buildings.description: Small Macehualtin household clusters for ordinary estate field labour. Cheap to build quickly, but inefficient to maintain for the capacity provided.

buildings.name: Medium Field Labour Ward
buildings.description: A more organised field-labour ward with shared support space. Higher upfront cost, but better building-upkeep efficiency.

buildings.name: Large Field Labour Compound
buildings.description: A substantial field-labour compound. Expensive to establish, but efficient for maintaining a large supported labour population.

buildings.name: Small Artisan Houses
buildings.description: Small Tolteca artisan housing close to workshops. Cheap early specialist capacity, but inefficient to maintain.

buildings.name: Medium Artisan Ward
buildings.description: A compact artisan ward with shared workshop-adjacent support. Higher upfront cost and better upkeep per supported artisan.

buildings.name: Large Artisan Compound
buildings.description: A major artisan compound for a mature workshop quarter. Expensive, but best long-term maintenance efficiency for Tolteca capacity.

buildings.name: Small Tlacotin Quarters
buildings.description: Basic quarters and supervision space for bonded or enslaved labour. Cheap capacity with relatively poor building-building-upkeep efficiency.

buildings.name: Medium Tlacotin Yard
buildings.description: A larger managed yard for Tlacotin labour. More costly to build, but better organised and cheaper to maintain per worker.

buildings.name: Large Tlacotin Compound
buildings.description: A major Tlacotin compound. High upfront cost, strong capacity, and the best comparative building-building-upkeep efficiency.

buildings.name: Small Warrior House
buildings.description: A small warrior support house. Useful for early war capacity but inefficient compared with larger warrior compounds.

buildings.name: Medium Warrior Lodge
buildings.description: A more organised warrior lodge with better storage, sleeping space and weapon support.

buildings.name: Large Warrior Compound
buildings.description: A large warrior compound for serious Flower Wars preparation. Expensive, but comparatively efficient for the capacity provided.

buildings.name: Small Priest House
buildings.description: Small priest housing and ritual support. Allows limited priest capacity at a manageable upfront cost.

buildings.name: Medium Priest Residence
buildings.description: A more stable residence for priests and ritual servants, with better comparative building-upkeep efficiency.

buildings.name: Large Ritual Residence
buildings.description: A substantial ritual residence. High upfront ritual cost, but efficient support for a larger priestly population.

buildings.name: Small Noble Residence
buildings.description: A modest noble residence. It supports a few nobles but has inefficient status upkeep.

buildings.name: Medium Noble Court
buildings.description: A formal noble court with better household organisation and improved support efficiency.

buildings.name: Large Noble Compound
buildings.description: A major noble compound. Very expensive, but the strongest long-term capacity and building-building-upkeep efficiency for Pipiltin.

buildings.name: Small Captive Holding Pen
buildings.description: A guarded holding pen for a small number of captives. Cheap to build but inefficient to maintain.

buildings.name: Medium Guarded Holding Yard
buildings.description: A larger guarded holding yard for captives taken in Flower Wars.

buildings.name: Large Captive Compound
buildings.description: A major captive compound. Expensive, but efficient if the house is regularly taking captives.
